_note: Curated from pylint-odoo v1.x for Odoo v9. Inherits v8 rules. Additions: @api.one deprecated, dual-manifest (__openerp__.py still primary), website/portal class checks.

rules.message: Manifest file is missing required key `name`.
Manifest file is missing required key `version`.
Manifest file is missing recommended key `author`.
Manifest file is missing recommended key `license`.
Manifest file `installable` key should be a boolean.
Use of deprecated `_columns` dict; prefer `fields.*` class attributes.
rules.fix_template: Replace `_columns = {'field': fields.Char(...)}` with class attribute `field = fields.Char(...)`.

rules.message: Use of deprecated `_constraints` list; use `@api.constrains` decorator.
Method uses old API `(cr, uid, ids, context)` signature; prefer new API `(self)`.
`@api.one` is deprecated in v9 — avoid; use `@api.multi` with self-iteration.
rules.fix_template: Replace `@api.one` with `@api.multi` and iterate `for rec in self:` inside the method.

rules.message: ir.model.access entry missing from security CSV — model may be inaccessible.
rules.fix_template: Ensure `security/ir.model.access.csv` lists all models defined in the module.

rules.message: XML id reference does not match any declared id in the module or its dependencies.
rules.fix_template: Verify the `ref='module.xml_id'` attribute points to a valid record id.

rules.message: Translation string uses positional `%s` formatting — use named `%(name)s` for clarity.
File declared in manifest `data` list does not exist.
Demo data file declared in manifest `demo` list does not exist.
SQL injection risk: `cr.execute` called with string interpolation instead of parameterized query.
Built-in expression evaluator used with external input — use `safe_eval()` from `openerp.tools` instead.
Hardcoded database ID used in code — use `env.ref()` instead.
`_rec_name` field not present in model fields.
`copy()` override does not call `super()` — may break duplication chain.
rules.fix_template: Ensure `res = super(MyModel, self).copy(default)` is called first.

rules.message: `write()` override does not call `super()` — changes may not be persisted.
`create()` override does not call `super()` — record will not be created.
